root_text.an3.1:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an3.1:2.1: “Yāni kānici, bhikkhave, bhayāni uppajjanti sabbāni tāni bālato uppajjanti, no paṇḍitato.
root_text.an3.1:2.2: Ye keci upaddavā uppajjanti sabbe te bālato uppajjanti, no paṇḍitato.
root_text.an3.1:2.3: Ye keci upasaggā uppajjanti sabbe te bālato uppajjanti, no paṇḍitato.
root_text.an3.1:2.4: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, naḷāgārā vā tiṇāgārā vā aggi mutto kūṭāgārānipi ḍahati ullittāvalittāni nivātāni phusitaggaḷāni pihitavātapānāni;
root_text.an3.1:2.5: evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, yāni kānici bhayāni uppajjanti sabbāni tāni bālato uppajjanti, no paṇḍitato.
root_text.an3.1:2.6: Ye keci upaddavā uppajjanti sabbe te bālato uppajjanti, no paṇḍitato.
root_text.an3.1:2.7: Ye keci upasaggā uppajjanti sabbe te bālato uppajjanti, no paṇḍitato.
root_text.an3.1:3.1: Iti kho, bhikkhave, sappaṭibhayo bālo, appaṭibhayo paṇḍito.
root_text.an3.1:3.4: Natthi, bhikkhave, paṇḍitato bhayaṁ, natthi paṇḍitato upaddavo, natthi paṇḍitato upasaggo.
root_text.an3.1:4.2: ‘yehi tīhi dhammehi samannāgato bālo veditabbo te tayo dhamme abhinivajjetvā, yehi tīhi dhammehi samannāgato paṇḍito veditabbo te tayo dhamme samādāya vattissāmā’ti.

translation_text.an3.1:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an3.1:1.3: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an3.1:2.1: “Whatever dangers there are, all come from the foolish, not from the astute.
translation_text.an3.1:2.2: Whatever perils there are, all come from the foolish, not from the astute.
translation_text.an3.1:2.3: Whatever hazards there are, all come from the foolish, not from the astute.
translation_text.an3.1:2.4: It’s like a fire that spreads from a hut made of reeds or grass, and burns down even a bungalow, plastered inside and out, draft-free, with doors fastened and windows shuttered.
translation_text.an3.1:2.5: In the same way, whatever dangers there are, all come from the foolish, not from the astute.
translation_text.an3.1:2.6: Whatever perils there are, all come from the foolish, not from the astute.
translation_text.an3.1:2.7: Whatever hazards there are, all come from the foolish, not from the astute.
translation_text.an3.1:3.1: So, the fool is dangerous, but the astute person is safe.
translation_text.an3.1:3.2: The fool is perilous, but the astute person is not.
translation_text.an3.1:3.3: The fool is hazardous, but the astute person is not.
translation_text.an3.1:3.4: There’s no danger, peril, or hazard that comes from the astute.
translation_text.an3.1:4.1: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an3.1:4.2: ‘We will shun the three things by which a fool is known, and we will undertake and follow the three things by which an astute person is known.’

variant_text.an3.1:2.4: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, naḷāgārā vā tiṇāgārā vā → naḷāgāro vā tiṇāgāro vā (bj) | aggi mutto → aggimukko (bj); aggi mukko (sya-all, km, pts1ed)
